Ultimately, this is why appeals to
scripture as a source of fixed moral
truth fail under scrutiny. History shows
that moral progress occurs not when
people obey inherited doctrines, but
when they question them. The values we
now consider self-evident, including
human dignity, equality, and freedom,
emerged because people chose reason,
empathy, and critical thinking over
unquestioned authority. That willingness
to challenge belief, not submit to it,
